channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former 'American Idol' hopeful arrested on child porn charges
item.description: Police say they found hundreds of images and videos of child pornography on a smartphone while searching his home.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Richardson/ KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana city wants federal help as more migrants arrive
item.description: "The federal government has got to step in, and they've got to start helping communities our size," the mayor said.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Resentenced 'juvenile lifer' can now get parole in 15 years
item.description: "What he did to her wasn't bad enough. He threw her away like trash. And he gets a chance to live a life," Ledford's stepmother Vicki Buitendyk said.
item.dc:creator: Byron Tollefson, NewsNation

item.title: Want to be a Power Ranger? Original costumes, props up for auction
item.description: From daggers, helmets, and suits, to blasters and even villains' costumes, the items being auctioned are as varied as the prices they are being sold for.
item.dc:creator: Marco Rosas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected drunken driver arrested after passing Harris' motorcade the wrong way in Wisconsin
item.description: A sheriff's official says a suspected drunken driver heading the wrong way passed within feet of Vice President Kamala Harris' motorcade following a campaign stop this week in Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A hurricane's historic destruction hasn't stopped North Carolina election workers and voters
item.description: HOT SPRINGS, N.C. (AP) — About three weeks after Hurricane Helene pummeled western North Carolina, the Hot Springs Community Center was still caked in mud. A paper sign hung on the door warning visitors to use “extreme caution” until the building could be inspected.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Republicans brace for leadership scramble
item.description: House Republicans are bracing for potential leadership shake-ups in the coming weeks, a post-election scramble that will heavily depend on who wins control of the lower chamber and White House in November. The biggest wildcard is what Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will do.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Alleged 'serial fraudster' faces multiple fraud, forgery charges
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) - A woman with an extensive criminal history was arrested and charged with multiple fraud, forgery and identity theft charges about two hours after she had been released from jail on Friday, Oct. 18, according to the Mesilla, New Mexico, Marshal's Office.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: Trump slams 'jerk' Obama as former president campaigns for Harris
item.description: The former president remains especially popular among Democrats, and he has used his appearances to try and appeal directly to Black men and to make the case that Trump is unfit to lead.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Abercrombie & Fitch chief Mike Jeffries arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, his romantic partner and a third man were arrested Tuesday on charges of luring men into drug-laced, outlandish and coercive sex parties by dangling the promise of modeling for the retailer's once-defining beefcake ads.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KRON4

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Selena Quintanilla, Missy Elliot, Steven Spielberg and more honored by Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday honored acclaimed filmmakers, singers, writers and others who have made their mark on American culture, awarding the prestigious National Medals of Arts and National Humanities Medals to 39 recipients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Judges reject GOP overseas ballot challenges in Michigan, North Carolina
item.description: State judges in Michigan and North Carolina on Monday rejected the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) separate challenges to certain overseas ballots in the key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted doesn't appear at state Capitol after subpoena
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted and then called to testify at the state Capitol did not show up Monday over growing pushback from state leaders who have blasted lawmakers' last-ditch tactics to subpoena Robert Roberson and spare his life for now.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NFL stadiums could lose $11 billion from climate threats by 2050: report
item.description: “This report is a wake-up call for the NFL, stadium owners, and local governments,” said Climate X CEO Lukky Ahmed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Eyewitness video captures frantic efforts to save lives after deadly collapse of dock walkway
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The metal gangway where dozens of people waited to board a ferry boat made a loud, creaking noise before snapping in the middle amid panicked cries from those sent plunging into the water.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge hears arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments displays in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge heard arguments at a hearing Monday on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta crew member reportedly made Marine remove 'threatening' shirt
item.description: A Marine Corps veteran from the San Francisco Bay Area was temporarily removed from a Delta flight on Wednesday by a crew member who deemed her T-shirt to be “threatening,” according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Starbucks releasing 'Wicked' drinks inspired by the upcoming film
item.description: Starbucks is releasing a pair of limited-edition drinks inspired by two main characters of the upcoming film, "Wicked.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Zenni Optical offering free glasses for those affected by Hurricane Helene, Milton
item.description: Zenni Optical, a Nothern California company that sells prescription glasses online, is offering free glasses for those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs faces 7 new lawsuits, including alleged rape of 13-year-old
item.description: Seven new lawsuits have been filed against Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs, including one alleging the rape of a 13-year-old girl. They come as his lawyers tried again Monday to get him freed on bail, and complained that a “fresh wave of publicity” is endangering his right to a fair criminal trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5
